front: Opera: Donizetti opera based on a Walter Scott novel, set in Scotland, depicting the feud between the Ravenswood and Ashton families
Opera: ends with the suicide of Edgardo at his ancestors' tomb after a duel challenge from Enrico at Wolf's Crag castle
Opera: Adelina Patti made her professional debut in the title role of this 1835 opera
Opera: its Mad Scene aria 'Il dolce suono' is often performed with a solo flute instead of the originally scored glass harmonica
Opera: the soprano appears in a bloodstained white dress after murdering her bridegroom Arturo on their wedding night, then hallucinates a wedding with Edgardo
Opera: the Mad Scene cabaletta 'Spargi d'amaro pianto' follows 'Il dolce suono'; earned a 12-minute ovation at Joan Sutherland's Met debut
Opera: its signature Mad Scene aria was written in F major for Fanny Tacchinardi Persiani but is usually transposed down to E-flat; the most popular cadenza was written by Mathilde Marchesi
Opera: Joan Sutherland's 1959 Covent Garden debut in this role, directed by Zeffirelli, made the character her signature role
Opera: a 2016 Covent Garden production by Katie Mitchell used a split-screen design by Vicki Mortimer to show the usually unseen murder of Arturo
Opera: in Act I, the title character tells her maid Alisa about a ghostly maiden who haunts a fountain — an ancestor's victim and omen of tragedy — in the aria 'Regnava nel silenzio'
Opera: 'Regnava nel silenzio' strikes its lowest note, a C-natural, on the first syllable of the phrase 'l'ombre mostrarsi'
Opera: in the sextet 'Chi mi frena,' the lead tenor removes the ring from Lucia's finger after discovering she has signed a marriage contract
